content.full_text:
110 WEST C STREET, SUITE 1709
SAN DIEGO, CA 92101
(619) 237-7844

1020 N STREET
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
(916) 445-5713

[SEAL]

ERNEST J. DRONENBURG, JR.
MEMBER, STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION

May 2, 1991


Mr. Mark Morodomi
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95812

Subject:  Mass Mailings and Distribution of the Annual Report for the
          Board of Equalization

Dear Mr. Morodomi:

     Pursuant to our conversations held over the last few weeks, I am
writing to confirm the resolution of my two questions.

     ISSUE I

     BACKGROUND:  Most of the 4,000 copies of our Agency's annual reports
are currently exempted from the Mass Mailings Regulation (Regulation
18901 of the Fair Political Practices Commission, Title 2, Division 6
of the California Code of Regulations).  Of those 4,000 copies, only
370 might come under the regulation (see Attachment 1).

     QUESTION:  If we coordinate the mailings of these 370 reports, so that
no more than 199 are mailed in a single calendar month, am I accurate
in my understanding that our agency would not violate any aspect of the
Mass Mailings Regulation and that the Annual Report may include Board
Member signatures, pictures, biographies, etc.?

     ISSUE II

     BACKGROUND:  The State Board of Equalization is made up of 4 elected
members each representing over 7 million Californians and by one
ex-officio member, the State Controller, who is elected statewide.  The
fact that our Board Members are elected and represent specified
districts makes them more analogous to the legislature concerning Mass
Mailings.  Currently, individual legislators may each mail up to 199
substantially similar pieces in a single calendar month and do not come
under the Mass Mailings Regulation.  Legislators are not all grouped
together and treated in the aggregate.  An example of this is the
annual printing and mailing of the California State Constitution by
members of the Assembly.  Many of these legislators mail out copies of
the constitution to their constituents.  They are not each limited to a
mailing of 2.5 copies per month because there are 80 members treated in
the aggregate.

Fair Political Practices Commission
Mass Mailings and Annual Reports
May 2, 1991
Page 2


QUESTION:  Am I accurate in my understanding that each elected Board
Member may send up to 199 copies of the Annual Report in a single
month and not violate any aspect of the Mass Mailings Regulation and
that the Annual Report may include Board Member signatures, pictures,
biographies, etc.?

I look forward to your written response.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Earl J. Cantos, Jr.
Deputy to Board Member

cc: Honorable Brad Sherman
    Honorable William Bennett
    Honorable Ernest J. Dronenburg, Jr.
    Honorable Matthew Fong
    Honorable Gray Davis
    Ms. Cindy Rambo
    Mr. Les Sorensen

ATTACHMENT 1


Approximate Numbers of Annual Reports Mass Mailed


EXEMPTION
UNDER REG.
18901:

(b)(4)     Board Excise Tax Unit ................................     75
(b)(4)     Board District Offices (statewide).................    600
(b)(4)     Board Headquarters (Sacramento Area)...............    266
(b)(3)     City Clerks...........................................    406
(b)(3)     County Assessors.....................................     58
(b)(3)     County Auditors.......................................     58
(b)(3)     Legislators...........................................     26
(a)(4)     Unsolicited Requests received by January 1*.......    500
(a)(4)     Unsolicited Requests received during the year*... 1,000

           TOTAL............................................................... 2,989


AREA AT
ISSUE:

           Board Member Offices
              1st District: Sacramento Office.........     10
              2nd District: Santa Monica Office.......     10
              3rd District: San Diego Office..........     50
              4th District: Lakewood Office...........    100
                           Sacramento Office..........    200

           TOTAL...................................................................    370


STORAGE:

           Annual Reports Saved for future use ...............    600

           TOTAL...................................................................    600

GRAND TOTAL.................................................... 3,959


*    "Unsolicited Requests" are individual requests submitted on special cards
and are signed by the requestor. Each annual report mailed contains a card
inquiring if the individual wishes to receive next year's report. Those who
wish to receive next year's annual report return the signed card. Cards are
accumulated throughout the year by our Communications Division, and the
reports are distributed following delivery.
